entities.name: daivī vāg
entities.cite: divine speech, the Sanskrit of the seed-tradition

entities.name: vyatikīrṇā
entities.cite: change as corruption, history as the silting of a pure source

entities.name: anityadarśin
entities.cite: for the seers of non-eternality, those whose darsana makes impermanence fundamental

entities.name: buddhiviparyayaḥ
entities.cite: in this dispute their judgement is the exact reverse

entities.name: vāda
entities.cite: the Sanskrit-Prakrit priority dispute is theory-laden all the way down

axioms.name: vyatikirana
axioms.cite: vyatikirnā, overspread, scattered through, asaktaih abhidhatrbhih, by speakers without capacity

axioms.name: pratikula_buddhi
axioms.cite: in this dispute their judgement is the exact reverse

axioms.name: viparyaya
axioms.cite: On their view the current speech of peoples is the natural datum and 'divine Sanskrit' the artifact — a fixed, frozen elaboration raised out of living vernaculars; the direction of derivation flips with the metaphysics

axioms.name: anisthaniya
axioms.cite: by speakers without capacity (the vigunas of 1.153)

claims.name: vyatikirana
claims.cite: This divine speech has been overspread by incapable speakers

claims.name: pratikula_buddhi
claims.cite: among the seers of non-eternality, in this matter, the judgement is the reverse

rejected_readings.label: refutation_reading
rejected_readings.rendering: The seers of non-eternality have a reversed and therefore false judgement about language.
rejected_readings.why: Projects refutation onto the text when the commentary explicitly states Bhartṛhari does not refute the opposite view. This violates the neutrality and structural honesty the verse embodies.

claims.name: asatya
claims.cite: The seers of non-eternality are wrong

rejected_readings.label: corruption_causation
rejected_readings.rendering: Incapable speakers caused the corruption of what was originally one unified language.
rejected_readings.why: Treats speakers as active agents responsible for corruption rather than simply operating within corrupted speech. The verse says the divine speech HAS BEEN overspread (passive voice), not that speakers actively corrupted it.

rejected_readings.label: modern_priority
rejected_readings.rendering: The seers of non-eternality are correct that modern speech is the natural source from which Sanskrit is derived.
rejected_readings.why: Treats the anti-eternalist position as epistemically privileged, when Bhartṛhari's insight is that both views flow from incompatible metaphysical commitments (eternality vs. impermanence), not from evidence alone.

claims.name: mula
claims.cite: modern speech is the natural source from which Sanskrit is derived

notes: The contract encodes the two competing frames (eternalist priority vs. anti-eternalist priority) as cognitions rather than absolute facts. Vyatikirana is rendered as manifestation (the state of being corrupted/overspread) rather than property, following the commentary's emphasis on historical process. The key insight is structural: Bhartṛhari presents both views without refutation, showing that the dispute is grounded in incompatible metaphysical commitments (eternality vs. impermanence), not in observable linguistic facts alone.
